original: The past few days have been rough: the pregnancy is hitting you with waves of nausea and heartburn. It’s like morning sickness, except it can come any time of the day or night. Strong smells can easily trigger it — even perfume — and many of the foods you love are now off-limits, much to your dismay. When you recognize the telltale signs, finding a bathroom (or another suitably private location, if you’re not in civilization) is all you have time to do before the retching starts. The solution so far has been to eat smaller meals and avoid certain kinds of food, especially if it’s fried, spicy or acidic. And when your stomach can’t seem to weather any food, you make do by drinking way more water than you normally would, which in turn means you’re going to the loo way more often... Well, your Codex says the pregnancy is progressing smoothly, so you’re sure this predicament will abate soon enough.
context: <<POS:36804859>> textify(['The past few days have been rough: the pregnancy i'...])

original: Thankfully, the nausea spells seem to be getting less and less frequent, however you’ve been feeling uncommonly tired and lazy as of late. original: You wager you’re about halfway through the pregnancy by this point, and the Braxton Hicks contractions have started — or at least you can feel them more clearly now. Thankfully, the cramps are very mild and sporadic: just a tightness in your lower abdomen, a sensation of squeezing and relaxing, as your uterine muscles exercise in preparation for labor.\n\n\t\t\tHowever, you’ve also been struck by dizziness and lightheadedness on occasion, and unfortunately those are not nearly as mild. Hot places and brusque changes in position can make your head spin, and sometimes you’re forced to sit down or lie on your side for a few minutes, until your blood flow regulates itself. So far, you’ve found that the best way to ameliorate those dizzy spells is to stay hydrated and eat frequent meals.

original: Recently, you’ve been feeling less tired, and the discomforts of pregnancy seem to have abated, if only for a while, so you’ve decided to use this interlude to prepare yourself for the challenge. Long hours of your time are dedicated to reading about what to expect moving forward: learning about maternity, watching child-birthing classes online and making a list of potential clinics or hospitals for the crucial day.\n\t\t\t\n\t\t\tGiven your interstellar activities, you’re never quite sure where you might wind up, so it pays to be prepared. Maybe Chance could lend a hand? You send him a message about this, to which he replies with contagious enthusiasm, offering to take care of the bureaucracy and assuring you that he’ll be there when the time comes, should you choose to give birth on the planet he’s currently at. Well, that was actually reassuring!

original: You nurse her peacefully for a while, lost in the throes of exhausted motherhood. At last her head droops against your arm, carried off into a deep sleep. You summon the drone, carefully sequester her into the climate-controlled device, and watch it bear your new child off to the nursery, where years of quiet safety await. You clean yourself up, and then disappear into a long, well-needed slumber yourself.
